sourceMovie.title: Alien Resurrection
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Alien Resurrection works as a science fiction movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The science fiction and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Resident Evil (2002) clearly understood what makes science-fiction work — the same quality that keeps Alien Resurrection rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Apocalypse
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Resident Evil: Apocalypse (2004) is the kind of film Alien Resurrection fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The science-fiction overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: The Terminator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Terminator shares Alien Resurrection's gift for science-fiction — the 1984 film delivers action without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Aliens
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Alien Resurrection compelling — the science-fiction, the action — shows up in Aliens (1986) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that tense storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Solo: A Star Wars Story
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The science-fiction energy of Alien Resurrection is alive in Solo: A Star Wars Story (2018), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a tense payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Predator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Predator (1987) operates in the same science-fiction as Alien Resurrection, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The tense payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Upgrade
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Upgrade (2018) belongs to the same conversation as Alien Resurrection — both anchor their science-fiction in action emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Akira
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Alien Resurrection's science-fiction stayed with you, Akira (1988) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The action through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Predators
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Alien Resurrection excels in science-fiction, Predators (2010) doubles down on action to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Riddick
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Riddick (2013) is the natural next step after Alien Resurrection: same science-fiction DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and action elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

faq.question: What should I watch after Alien Resurrection?
faq.answer: Try Resident Evil, Resident Evil: Apocalypse, The Terminator—each shares DNA with Alien Resurrection while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Alien Resurrection?
faq.answer: Alien Resurrection (1997) is best described as Science Fiction, Horror, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Alien Resurrection part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Alien Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
